title: Evangelions wholly angel
wikitext:
[]: Evangelions, wholly angel?             Quote Post by jayngfet » Tue Nov 10, 2009 8:09 pm So, I've been searching through the wiki and found a few different images of the failed EVA prototypes, then compared the looks of other evangelions and wondered, were these direct copies of angels or did NERV and SEELE add something else into the mix? Adam and Lilith, from what we see, are mostly humanoid, though their facial features AT LEAST look nothing like the EVA's. Meanwhile dispite being based supposedly solely on mostly one creature the Adam based EVA's vary greatly among themselves, eyes ranging from one(failed prototype), to seven(another failure) and more. I'm proposing that at some point NERV added something "else" to the evangelions during creation. I'd bet there's a little bit of human in there(possibly hoping to either make EVA's control better or boost synchronization rates), possibly a bit of certain animals and micro organisms(maybe to augment size or speed, who knows). What I'm saying is, could NERV and SEELE have messed with the EVA's to that extent, changing their genetic material in that kind of way?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Nov 10, 2009 8:25 pm What we know for certain is that the single biggest difference between Evas and Angels was what Nerv didn't add. Specifically, the S^2 engine which provides Angels with seemingly limitless energy. Without it, the Eva's have to rely on electrical power via cables and batteries. There are certainly other modifications that have been made. Cabling for power, electronics, sensors. Rivets and the like to hold the armour and components in place. A big alteration was the entire entry plug complex, designed to subordinate the Evas to a pilots mental control (via the "A-10" nerve whatever that is). Then of course there are the "restraints" or "shoulder pylons", designed to keep the Evas from unleashing their true potential, until the time was deemed right. But you are referring to other "organic" alterations Nerv may have made. The most significant of these is probably the implement of a human soul within the Evas (This is probably counts as organic in the context of Eva metaphysiology). I don't think any real "genetic" manipulation occured beyond this. Though, who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Tue Nov 10, 2009 8:46 pm Yeah, NERV made significant modifications to the EVA bodies, restraints, cybernetics, armor, and a bunch of other things, biological modifications would seem to be par for the course. Another thing to consider is the EVA's lack S2 organs for an unknown reason. Maybe they were modified to the point of being pilot able, but this somehow made it impossible to make a functioning organ?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Nov 10, 2009 9:30 pm Another fact could be that their cloning process seems to be less than perfect. All the clones that we see in the series aren't exact copies of what they are cloned from. ObsessiveMathsFreak makes a good point with the souls. Both Rei and Kaworu have red eyes, so perhaps this physical characteristic is due to them having the souls of SoL inside them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 10, 2009 10:11 pm 1) Clone Adam (Or in one case, Lilith) 2) Castrate them and remove/prevent any S2 Engine presence. 3) Cyberneticize them into cyborgs. 4) Add a soul. 5) Congrats, you have an Evangelion. ObsessiveMathsFreak makes a good point with the souls. Both Rei and Kaworu have red eyes, so perhaps this physical characteristic is due to them having the souls of SoL inside them.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Nov 10, 2009 10:17 pm Good point. By matter they are made of, I'm guessing you're referring to PWM? My money is still on "Nerv sucks at making clones".

[]:             Quote Post by knightrous » Thu Nov 12, 2009 9:51 am Maybe they weren't technically cloned. They might be composed of the same genetic materials, but they can still form differently. Same with humans, we are all composed of human DNA, but we are all quite different. Even Twins still have variences despite being the closest thing to natural cloning.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 12, 2009 10:29 am AuraTwilight wrote: 1) Clone Adam (Or in one case, Lilith) 2) Castrate them and remove/prevent any S2 Engine presence. 3) Cyberneticize them into cyborgs. 4) Add a soul. 5) Congrats, you have an Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Nov 12, 2009 10:32 am It should be noted that the Angels and presumably Evas do not have regular DNA molecules, i.e. those consisting of hydrocarbons. Their "DNA" is made of of Particle Wave Matter (PWM) and has only a similar structure and pattern to Human DNA. That is to say, while they are made from completely different things, there is an isomorphism between the Angel Code and Human DNA sequences.

[]:             Quote Post by MetalGearFlaccid » Mon Nov 23, 2009 1:55 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: It should be noted that the Angels and presumably Evas do not have regular DNA molecules, i.e. those consisting of hydrocarbons. Their "DNA" is made of of Particle Wave Matter (PWM) and has only a similar structure and pattern to Human DNA. That is to say, while they are made from completely different things, there is an isomorphism between the Angel Code and Human DNA sequences.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 26, 2009 3:46 pm AuraTwilight wrote: 2) Castrate them and remove/prevent any S2 Engine presence.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 26, 2009 5:55 pm The couldn't get S2 engines with cloning. That's why artificial ones were made.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Dec 03, 2009 3:07 am pretty sure the s2 organs came from studying Shamshel's leftovers

[]:             Quote Post by Layla » Thu Dec 03, 2009 4:57 pm Axell wrote: pretty sure the s2 organs came from studying Shamshel's leftovers

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Thu Dec 03, 2009 8:00 pm Layla wrote: Axell wrote: pretty sure the s2 organs came from studying Shamshel's leftovers

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Dec 04, 2009 4:53 am That, and it's implied the S2 Engine in Unit 04 was deliberately sabotaged by SEELE, meaning they already understood how it worked.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamina » Sat Dec 05, 2009 11:05 am A few of you have confused me a bit. I always thought that the Eva units were simply clones of Adam or Lilith depending minus the S2 engine. Then they encased them in armor and restraints that ran on power and thus were unable to really do much unless the armor functioned normally. I always thought the synthetic creature could of moved regardless of power but because of the nature of its restraints and armor it simply didn't have the strength to move unless it took a S2 engine into itself. So if the armor was not powered, the synthetic being inside could not move normally because the armor and restraints had locked up making movement very difficult. This would of been a logical decision on the part of Nerve in order to keep them under control as much as possible, as a weapon that runs on limited power serves a disadvantage, but also makes it more safe and controlled. This would explain why unit one broke the restraints off of its body in episode 16 before really going after Zeruel to finish it off after going berserk.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Dec 05, 2009 2:35 pm Kamina wrote: I always thought the synthetic creature could of moved regardless of power but because of the nature of its restraints and armor it simply didn't have the strength to move unless it took a S2 engine into itself.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Sun Dec 06, 2009 3:05 am AuraTwilight wrote: That, and it's implied the S2 Engine in Unit 04 was deliberately sabotaged by SEELE, meaning they already understood how it worked.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Dec 06, 2009 5:26 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: To be honest, I have never been sure exactly what the Evas need power for, or exactly what it is that the S^2 organ even powers. Unit-01 still runs off electrical power in #23', though its run in #24' is long enough to be ambiguous. However in #25' there appears to a power shutdown, however the plug screens remain on.
